questions.text: What will be the retention period for an object if the parameter MIN_DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS is set at the account level and the DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS is set at the object level?

options.a: The retention period will be equal to the value of the parameter DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the object level.
options.b: The retention period will be equal to the greatest of the two values of parameters DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the object level, and MIN_DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the account level.
options.c: The retention period will be equal to the value of the parameter MIN_DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the account level.
options.d: The retention period will be equal to the lesser of the two values of parameters DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the object level and MIN_DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS set at the account level

questions.explanation: Snowflake ensure compliance by using the larger value between the object-level setting and the account-level minimum floor.

questions.text: What is the MINIMUM Snowflake edition required to create a materialized view?
questions.explanation: Materialized views are an optimization feature restricted to Enterprise edition and higher.

questions.text: What's the default size for a newly-provisioned Snowpark-optimized virtual warehouse?
questions.explanation: Snowpark-optimized warehouses, which provide more memory per node, default to a Medium size.

questions.text: Which Snowflake feature enables loading data from cloud storage as soon as files are available in a stage?
questions.explanation: Snowpipe uses event notifications to ingest data in micro-batches immediately upon file arrival in a stage.

questions.text: How is unstructured data managed in Snowflake?

options.a: Snowflake stores unstructured data in external cloud storage containers and uses third-party tools to query data.
options.b: Snowflake natively stores, manages and supports queries of unstructured data within the platform.
options.c: Snowflake requires the use of server-side encryption on internal stages containing unstructured data.
options.d: Unstructured data must be converted to structured data before it can be placed into Snowflake storage.

questions.explanation: Unstructured data management relies on stages (internal or external) and directory tables to store and process files.

questions.text: By default, which Snowflake role has the ability to manage grants?
questions.explanation: SECURITYADMIN is the system role that inherently possesses the MANAGE GRANTS privilege.

questions.text: Which Snowflake objects can be defined with future grants? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Future grants allow you to pre-define permissions for objects not yet created within a specific database or schema.

questions.text: If a network policy is applied at both the account and user levels, which policy takes precedence?

options.d: The policy that was applied first

questions.explanation: Snowflake prioritizes the most specific policy; a user-level network policy overrides the account-level setting.

questions.text: Which function is used to retrieve the filename for a file stored on a stage?
questions.explanation: GET_RELATIVE_PATH is used to extract the file path relative to the stage, which identifies the specific file.

questions.text: What information in the Query Profile can be used to improve the performance of the query when it is run again?

options.a: Use the recommended SQL syntax changes that will improve partition pruning.
options.b: Identify which parts of the query were most resource intensive.
options.d: Follow the recommended virtual warehouse configuration changes to avoid memory spillage.

questions.explanation: The Query Profile provides detailed execution statistics to pinpoint bottlenecks like heavy scans or joins.

questions.text: Which function will convert semi-structured data to a relational data representation?
questions.explanation: FLATTEN is a table function used to unnest hierarchical data into a relational row/column format.

questions.text: Which Snowpark element represents a data set and provides ways to operate on the data?
questions.explanation: DataFrames are the primary structure in Snowpark for defining and executing data transformations.

questions.text: Which virtual warehouse configuration requires the maximum number of clusters to be equal to the minimum number of clusters?
questions.explanation: Maximized mode forces a constant number of clusters by setting MIN and MAX to the same value.

questions.text: Which database objects can be shared using Secure Data Sharing? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Tables and Dynamic Tables are among the primary objects that can be included in a Snowflake share.

questions.text: What statement describes Snowflake data architecture?
questions.explanation: Snowflake is characterized by its separation of storage and compute, creating a multi-cluster shared data environment.

questions.text: What metadata is stored for each micro-partition? (Select TWO)

options.b: The clustering key used in the table.
options.c: The average depth of values for each of the columns.
options.d: The range of values for each of the columns in the full table.
options.e: The minimum and maximum values for each of the columns.

questions.explanation: Snowflake stores min/max values and distinct counts for every column within each micro-partition to enable pruning.

questions.text: What does creating a resource monitor for a virtual warehouse allow a user to do?

options.a: Schedule when the warehouse starts and stops.
options.b: Track and control the credits consumed by the warehouse.
options.c: Monitor the real-time performance of all queries running on the warehouse.
options.d: Automatically adjust the settings of the warehouse based on the workload.

questions.explanation: Resource monitors track credit usage and can trigger actions like suspension to prevent overspending.

questions.text: Which models are supported for Snowflake access control? (Select TWO).
questions.explanation: Snowflake access control is a hybrid of Discretionary (DAC) and Role-Based (RBAC) models.

questions.text: Which connectors support Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) token caching when connecting to Snowflake? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: The JDBC and Python connectors support caching tokens to streamline subsequent MFA-protected logins.

questions.text: Who can access the results of a previously-executed query?

options.a: The user who executed the query.
options.b: The role that executed the query.

questions.explanation: Query result cache access is scoped to the specific role that originally executed the query.

questions.text: A company stores Personal Identifiable Information (PII) in Snowflake across multiple countries, including the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. How can Snowflake be used to automatically identify data, in order to support data governance requirements for each country?

options.a: Tag all columns directly that contain PII.
options.d: Implement a third-party service to protect the data.

questions.explanation: Classification analyzes data using ML to proposed semantic categories like PII to aid in discovery and protection.

questions.text: What kind of values does a User-Defined Function (UDF) return? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: UDFs return either a single scalar value per input or a set of rows (tabular/UDTF).

questions.text: How can storage costs be MINIMIZED while maintaining the history and integrity of a table?

options.a: Decrease the Time Travel retention period.
options.b: Convert the table type to transient.
options.c: Use zero-copy clones of the table.

questions.explanation: Zero-copy clones share the same micro-partitions as the original, incurring no additional storage costs until the data is modified.

questions.text: Which Snowflake object will consume credits during automatic background maintenance?
questions.explanation: Materialized views consume credits for the background serverless service that keeps them refreshed.

questions.text: In a SPLIT_PART function, what will the returned value be if the partNumber is out of range?
questions.explanation: SPLIT_PART returns an empty string if the requested part index is higher than the actual parts found.

questions.text: If file format options or copy options are specified in multiple locations, which location will take precedence?
questions.explanation: Options defined directly in the COPY statement override those set at the stage, table, or schema levels.

questions.text: What Snowflake feature or service is used to prevent third-parties from reading data while in transit?
questions.explanation: TLS encryption ensures that data is protected from interception as it travels between clients and Snowflake.

questions.text: Which Snowflake features or services are designed to support data recovery without impacting ongoing operations? (Select TWO)

options.d: Local caching of data within virtual warehouses

questions.explanation: Time Travel provides user-managed recovery, while Fail-safe acts as a backup managed by Snowflake support.

questions.text: What will happen if multiple identical COPY_INTO <table> statements, using the default settings, are executed at the same time?

options.a: The statements will load the data only once in the table.
options.b: The statements will duplicate the data in the table.
options.c: The statements will fail with an error relating to reading the same file.
options.d: The statements will overwrite the data in the table.

questions.explanation: Snowflake's load history tracking prevents duplicate files from being ingested twice unless explicitly forced.

questions.text: What triggers the automated maintenance of a table's clustering key after it has been defined?

options.a: A scheduled task established by the ORGADMIN.
options.b: A time-based schedule set by the user.
options.c: A Snowflake determination based on the table size.
options.d: A Snowflake determination that the table will benefit from maintenance.

questions.explanation: The automatic clustering service triggers re-clustering based on Snowflake's assessment of whether it will improve query efficiency.

questions.text: A security review identified several roles that are no longer being used. Which of the roles can be dropped?
questions.explanation: System roles like USERADMIN, PUBLIC, and ACCOUNTADMIN are permanent; custom roles like FINANCEADMIN can be dropped.

questions.text: How can a user access information about a query execution plan without consuming virtual warehouse compute resources?

options.c: Review the data in the Account_Usage view.

questions.explanation: EXPLAIN compiles the query and outputs the execution plan without using a virtual warehouse.

questions.text: What happens to event notifications that are received when an automated Snowpipe is paused?

options.a: They are immediately dropped from the internal metadata.
options.b: They are stored indefinitely in the internal metadata until the pipe is manually resumed.
options.c: They are permanently stored in the internal metadata and processed once the pipe is resumed.
options.d: They enter a retention period of 14 days, after which they are dropped from the internal metadata.

questions.explanation: Notifications received while Snowpipe is paused have a 14-day lifespan before being discarded from metadata.

questions.text: What does the VALUE column represent in the output of a FLATTEN function used as a parameter when loading data into Snowflake?

options.a: The key to the exploded value
options.b: The path to the data element that needs to be flattened
options.c: An index of all of the elements that are in an ARRAY
options.d: The content of the element of a flattened ARRAY or OBJECT

questions.explanation: The VALUE column in FLATTEN contains the data element (value) for the current array index or object key.

questions.text: Which authentication method requires access to a secure file that is only stored on the user's local device?
questions.explanation: Key-pair authentication requires an RSA private key file stored on the user's computer.

questions.text: A query of data used in a dashboard is performing poorly. The query aggregates data that only changes at the end of the business day, recalculating every time the dashboard is viewed. How can the query performance be improved?

options.a: Ensure that only the columns needed in the dashboard are selected.
options.b: Recommend that a dedicated warehouse be used to view the dashboard.
options.c: Create a row access policy so users see only selected data.
options.d: Create a materialized view of the query.

questions.explanation: Materialized views pre-calculate aggregations, making them much faster for dashboards with stable daily data.

questions.text: When unloading data, setting which COPY_INTO <location> parameter will ensure that string fields in tables with zero length will be treated as NULL values?
questions.explanation: The NULL_IF parameter defines what character sequence in the source is converted to a NULL in the output.

questions.text: How does Snowflake support Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) with a second factor device?

options.d: Using federated authentication and Single Sign-On (SSO)

questions.explanation: Snowflake MFA uses Duo Security and time-based codes (TOTP) or push notifications.

questions.text: When using COPY INTO <location> command to generate CSV files from data in a Snowflake table, which parameter is used to create the files in a GZIP compression format?
questions.explanation: AUTO compression in COPY INTO defaults to GZIP format for unloaded files.

questions.text: Which least-privileged role is required to create a resource monitor?
questions.explanation: Resource monitor management is an account-level administrative task assigned to ACCOUNTADMIN by default.

questions.text: At what levels can a network policy be configured in Snowflake? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Network policies can be applied globally at the Account level or to specific Users.

questions.text: How can a Data Exchange Administrator provide a user with account access to a Data Exchange?

options.a: Grant the user the USERADMIN role
options.b: Add the user to the Data Exchange
options.c: Enable the IMPORT SHARE privilege and grant this privilege to the user.
options.d: Create a new database for the Data Exchange and provide access to the user.

questions.explanation: Data Exchange membership is managed by adding specific accounts/users to the group by an administrator.

questions.text: Which Snowflake data governance feature supports the tracking of sensitive data for compliance, discovery, protection, and resource usage?
questions.explanation: Data classification uses ML to discover and track PII and other sensitive data attributes.

questions.text: Which function estimates the number of distinct values in a column without scanning the entire table?
questions.explanation: APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT uses probabilistic estimation to calculate distinct counts efficiently.

questions.text: What is the minimum Snowflake edition required to use object tagging?
questions.explanation: Object tagging is a governance feature introduced in the Enterprise edition.

questions.text: If a multi-cluster virtual warehouse is running in Auto-scale mode, how are workloads with unpredictable usage patterns handled?

options.a: Snowflake automatically resizes the warehouse based on usage requirements.
options.b: Snowflake automatically starts and stops additional clusters as needed.
options.c: Snowflake notifies users when to adjust the number of clusters to improve workload performance.
options.d: Snowflake automatically starts additional clusters when the primary cluster reaches 50% capacity.

questions.explanation: Auto-scale mode handles varying load by dynamically adjusting the number of active clusters.

questions.text: What is the result of running this SQL command? ALTER WAREHOUSE MY_WH SET AUTO_SUSPEND = 0

options.a: The warehouse MY_WH will never suspend.
options.b: The warehouse MY_WH will suspend immediately after every query execution.
options.c: The warehouse MY_WH will suspend when the first query completes, and will not resume.
options.d: The query will fail with an error that says that there is an invalid value for AUTO_SUSPEND.

questions.explanation: Setting AUTO_SUSPEND to 0 disables automatic suspension for that warehouse.

questions.text: What languages can be used to write User-Defined Functions (UDF's)? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: JavaScript and Python are two native languages supported for Snowflake UDF logic.

questions.text: When would creating and using a standard view be preferable to using a materialized view?

options.a: The underlying query of the view is run on large data sets.
options.b: The underlying query of the view takes a lot of time to run.
options.d: The underlying query of the view is resource-intensive.

questions.explanation: Standard views are better for frequently changing data to avoid the high re-clustering/maintenance costs of materialized views.

questions.text: Which Snowflake object type can be used to restrict users from viewing the DDL associated with an object?
questions.explanation: Secure views hide the internal DDL and SQL logic from users without OWNERSHIP privileges.

questions.text: How does resizing a virtual warehouse affect Snowflake billing?

options.a: Each time a warehouse is resized, 1 credit is billed.
options.b: Each time a warehouse is resized, each second of usage is billed.
options.c: A warehouse can be resized an unlimited number of times without consuming any credits.
options.d: When a warehouse is resized to a larger size it is billed for 1 minute of usage.

questions.explanation: Resizing is billed by the second based on the active size of the compute resource.

questions.text: Which Snowflake native tool can be used to diagnose and troubleshoot network connections?
questions.explanation: SnowCD (Snowflake Connectivity Diagnostic) specifically tests your network connection to Snowflake endpoints.

questions.text: Which step will reduce costs when backing up a table?

options.b: Unload the table data to a stage
options.c: Share the table data with an account in another region
options.d: Copy the table using a Create Table as Select (CTAS) statement

questions.explanation: Cloning uses metadata pointers and doesn't duplicate storage costs until data is actually changed.

questions.text: Which commands in a query will benefit from the Query Acceleration Service? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: QAS accelerates operations involving large scans like complex INSERTs and CTAS statements.

questions.text: What are the benefits of using a persisted query results in Snowflake? (Select TWO)

options.a: Optimization of queries that select from hybrid tables.
options.b: Reduction of the time it takes to run a query.
options.c: Optimization of queries that include external functions.
options.d: Availability of query results for additional processing.
options.e: Query performance optimization when table micro-partitions have changed.

questions.explanation: Persisted result cache allows for instantaneous repeat queries and post-processing using RESULT_SCAN.

questions.text: What is the MAXIMUM data retention period for transient databases when using the Snowflake Enterprise edition?
questions.explanation: Transient objects are limited to 1 day of Time Travel retention and no Fail-safe.

questions.text: What tasks should be performed with SnowSQL? (Select TWO)

options.a: Sharing data with customers that do not have a Snowflake account.
options.b: Running queries and performing Data Definition Language (DDL) and Data Manipulation Language (DML) operations.
options.d: Loading and unloading data into tables.
options.e: Visualizing data with graphs and charts.

questions.explanation: SnowSQL is the command-line interface for executing SQL and moving data via PUT/GET.

questions.text: Which Snowflake objects can have a clustering key? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Clustering keys can be defined on permanent tables and materialized views to improve performance.

questions.text: The ORDERS table contains 12000 rows. Which query will return 10 rows that are randomly sampled from the table every time the query is run?
questions.explanation: The TABLESAMPLE clause is used specifically for random sampling of records.

questions.text: On which Snowflake objects can a Data Metric Function (DMF) be set? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: DMFs for data quality monitoring can be applied to standard, hybrid, and dynamic tables.

questions.text: Use of which features or objects will incur Snowflake storage costs? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Storage costs apply to permanent table data (Fail-safe included) and transient table data.

questions.text: Which resource monitor setting will cancel all active queries in a virtual warehouse when the threshold is reached?
questions.explanation: SUSPEND_IMMEDIATE cancels all running queries as soon as the credit threshold is hit.

questions.text: When is a DataFrame processed in Snowpark?

options.b: When a collect() action is triggered.
options.c: When there is sufficient memory available in the virtual warehouse.
options.d: When a new transformation is applied on the DataFrame.

questions.explanation: Snowpark follows lazy execution; work is only performed when an action like collect() is called.

questions.text: While unloading a Snowflake table data into a CSV file, a user notes that float column values are being truncated. Use of which data format would preserve the float column values?
questions.explanation: Parquet is a binary format that maintains the original precision of floating-point numbers.

questions.text: Which Snowflake service is managed within the Cloud Services layer?
questions.explanation: The Cloud Services layer manages account infrastructure, metadata, and authentication.

questions.text: What sequence of commands will unload data into a local directory?
questions.explanation: Data must be copied to a stage (COPY INTO), verified (LIST), and then downloaded locally (GET).

questions.text: What is the MINIMUM Snowflake edition required to add masking policies to selectively mask plain-text data in a table?
questions.explanation: Dynamic Data Masking requires Enterprise edition or higher.

questions.text: Which data type can be used for floating-point numbers without losing precision?
questions.explanation: NUMBER (Fixed-point) stores exact values with a specified precision (up to 38 digits).

questions.text: Which object can be used to query the data loading history for the last 365 days for a Snowflake account?
questions.explanation: ACCOUNT_USAGE.COPY_HISTORY stores a long-term (365-day) record of all file ingestion.

questions.text: If a table was dropped and the DATA_RETENTION_TIME_IN_DAYS was set to 0, how can the data from the table be recovered?

options.b: By creating a Snowflake Support ticket.
options.c: By running the UNDROP TABLE command.
options.d: By running the CLONE... BEFORE command.

questions.explanation: Without Time Travel, recovery must go through Fail-safe via Snowflake Support.

questions.text: How can unstructured data be processed in a Snowflake stage? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Python UDFs can read file streams from stages, and external functions can process file metadata/URLs.

questions.text: While working with unstructured data, which file function generates a Snowflake-hosted file URL to a staged file using the user session?
questions.explanation: Scoped URLs are session-specific and restricted to the current user's role.

questions.text: When loading JSON files that contain NULL values, which file format option should be set to = TRUE?
questions.explanation: STRIP_NULL_VALUES removes fields with null values, allowing them to be handled as missing in the VARIANT column.

questions.text: Which transformation operations can be used when loading data into a table using the COPY_INTO <table> command?

options.a: Using a JOIN or GROUP BY aggregation.
options.b: Using column reordering and column omission operations.
options.c: Filtering the results of a FROM clause using a WHERE clause
options.d: Using the ORDER BY, LIMIT, FETCH and TOP keywords in SELECT statements

questions.explanation: COPY INTO allows basic projections like column selection, casting, and reordering.

questions.text: What data type should be used to store hierarchical semi-structured data in Snowflake?
questions.explanation: VARIANT is the universal data type for storing all hierarchical semi-structured formats like JSON.

questions.text: What is run with a CALL statement?
questions.explanation: Stored procedures are standalone blocks of logic executed using the CALL command.

questions.text: Using what type of table will help MINIMIZE storage costs for tables that contain non-critical data and change frequently?
questions.explanation: Transient tables avoid Fail-safe costs, making them cheaper for high-churn, non-critical data.

questions.text: A size X-Small virtual warehouse ran for 90 seconds, and was shut down. The warehouse was then run for another 30 seconds before being shut down again. How many seconds will be billed?
questions.explanation: Total usage is 120s (90s + 30s), exceeding the 60s minimum on the first run.

questions.text: What command will return a list of files that have been unloaded to a user stage?
questions.explanation: LIST is the standard command for showing files in a Snowflake stage.

questions.text: Which function will return the URL for an external stage by using the stage name as the input?
questions.explanation: GET_STAGE_LOCATION returns the cloud storage bucket location linked to a specific stage.

questions.text: Which database object can be created as temporary for a given session?
questions.explanation: Temporary stages can be defined that exist only for the life of the current session.

questions.text: Which keywords can be used interchangeably when performing data sampling in Snowflake? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: SAMPLE and TABLESAMPLE are equivalent, and BERNOULLI is a type of row-based sampling.

questions.text: What is a fundamental characteristic of Snowflake micro-partitions?

options.a: They can be read directly as files.
options.b: They serve as an index for Snowflake tables.
options.c: They are sized based on Time Travel requirements.
options.d: Once established, they cannot be changed.

questions.explanation: Snowflake micro-partitions are immutable; modifications always result in the writing of new partitions.

questions.text: Which option will replace NULL values with an empty string while unloading data into a file?
questions.explanation: The NULL_IF parameter specifies the string representation of SQL NULLs in unloaded files.

questions.text: What would be the outcome of querying a VARIANT column using an invalid path?
questions.explanation: Queries to missing keys in a VARIANT object return a SQL NULL.

questions.text: What is the MINIMUN role required to create database objects?
questions.explanation: SYSADMIN is the intended role for standard object creation and ownership.

questions.text: Which Snowflake feature allows data providers to share data with consumers who do not have Snowflake accounts?
questions.explanation: Reader accounts provide a Snowflake managed instance for third parties without their own account.

questions.text: When using COPY INTO <location> command to generate CSV files from data in a Snowflake table, which option would compress the file using the default algorithm?
questions.explanation: AUTO compression defaults to GZIP when unloading data to stages.

questions.text: Which virtual warehouse configuration enables horizontal scaling?
questions.explanation: Horizontal scaling specifically refers to adding clusters to handle more concurrent queries.

questions.text: How can a data provider deliver data privately to multiple data consumers, at no cost for the consumers?

options.d: Unload the data to external storage

questions.explanation: Data Exchanges facilitate private sharing where providers can subsidize consumer costs.

questions.text: Which object or feature can be used to monitor access to shared data?
questions.explanation: ACCESS_HISTORY tracks data access across the account, including shared databases.

questions.text: How can queries be identified that would benefit from the query acceleration service?

options.a: Review the Query Profile to assess the effectiveness of the query pruning.

questions.explanation: This view identifies historical queries that the service could have improved.

questions.text: In Snowflake, what allows users to perform recursive queries?
questions.explanation: CONNECT BY is the native clause used for recursive hierarchy traversal in Snowflake.

questions.text: What parameter setting will MAXIMIZE parallel processing when unloading data from Snowflake?
questions.explanation: SINGLE = FALSE allows Snowflake to use multiple threads to write multiple files simultaneously.

questions.text: For how long is data history reported in the QUERY_HISTORY view?
questions.explanation: QUERY_HISTORY in the Account Usage schema maintains up to 365 days of record.

questions.text: How are clusters handled when the Economy scaling policy is set on a multi-cluster virtual warehouse?

options.a: A new cluster starts when the system detects that there is one or more queries queued waiting for an available cluster
options.b: A new cluster will start every 60 seconds if there are queued queries that have been waiting for more than 30 seconds
options.c: A new cluster starts only if the system estimates that the queued queries will require resources that will keep the new cluster
options.d: A cluster will shut down after 3 system checks indicating that the cluster has been under-utilized for a minimum of 6 minutes

questions.explanation: Economy policy prioritizes conservation, only starting clusters if it anticipates sustained work for them.

questions.text: When would the use of the query acceleration service be MOST beneficial? (Select TWO)

options.a: When most queries have no filters or aggregate clauses
options.b: When analytical queries are performed frequently at unschedule times
options.c: When queries typically include a LIMIT clause without an ORDER BY clause
options.d: When queries with small scans and highly-selective filters are run frequently
options.e: When the data workloads for most queries have large, unpredictable data volumes

questions.explanation: The service is ideal for handling sporadic, large scan workloads efficiently.

questions.text: Which table function will automatically detect the file metadata schema for a set of staged semi-structured data files, and then retrieve the column definitions?
questions.explanation: INFER_SCHEMA analyzes staged files (Parquet, Avro, etc.) to determine their internal structure.

questions.text: What does an integration between Snowflake and Microsoft Private Link or AWS PrivateLink support?

options.a: The isolation of data within a Snowflake account
options.b: The use of Secure Data Sharing among Snowflake accounts
options.c: A Virtual Private Network (VPN) between a user and Snowflake
options.d: A secure, direct connection to Snowflake that does not use the internet

questions.explanation: PrivateLink enables private network connectivity directly to Snowflake without using the public internet.

questions.text: Which step will increase security related to user access to a Snowflake account?

options.a: Set up a User-Defined Function (UDF) to run a daily report identifying all security-related session parameters used in the last 24 hours
options.b: Restrict access to Snowflake to known networks using a network policy
options.c: Establish a very short session timeout parameter
options.d: Default all new users to a PUBLIC role with restrictive permissions

questions.explanation: Network policies protect account access by limiting incoming requests to trusted IP ranges.

questions.text: On which objects can the REMOVE command be applied? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: REMOVE (RM) is used to delete files from both internal and external stages.

questions.text: What can be used to improve the performance of queries that are run on an external table?
questions.explanation: A materialized view caches external data within Snowflake storage, bypassing slower cloud object store scans.

questions.text: Which Snowflake table stores file-level metadata about unstructured data files in a stage, including the size, last-modified timestamp, and Snowflake file URL?
questions.explanation: Directory tables serve as built-in catalogs for unstructured data file metadata on stages.

questions.text: Which Query Profile operator can be used to identify inefficient micro-partition pruning?
questions.explanation: TableScan metrics show total versus scanned partitions, revealing how effectively Snowflake pruned the data.

questions.text: Which feature uses a Snowflake key with a customer-managed key to protect data?
questions.explanation: Tri-Secret Secure combines account-level, data-level, and user-managed keys for multi-layered protection.

questions.text: Which combination of permissions are required to create a pipe in Snowflake? (Select TWO)

options.b: CREATE PIPE at the schema level
options.c: CREATE SCHEMA at the database level
options.d: OWNERSHIP on the table in the pipe definition
options.e: SELECT and INSERT on the table in the pipe definition

questions.explanation: A pipe requires the permission to create the object and the ability to interact with the target table data.

questions.text: A global company needs to share financial reports with managers who are in different regions. Managers should only see the data from their respective regions. How can these requirements be met with the LEAST amount of operational overhead?

options.a: Create unique views for each regions
options.b: Build individual reports for each manager
options.c: User row-level security on the source data table used to generate the reports
options.d: Apply Dynamic Data Masking based on a custom role assigned to each manager

questions.explanation: Row Access Policies provide dynamic filtering based on user attributes, avoiding the need for multiple objects.

questions.text: A user reports poor query performance when running a SEARCH_IP query. How can performance be improved?

options.a: Alter the warehouse and set the maximum cluster count to 3
options.b: Alter the warehouse to enable the query acceleration service
options.c: Alter the table to add search optimization on the IP column
options.d: Alter the table to add clustering on the IP column

questions.explanation: Search Optimization is designed to accelerate point-lookups like IP searches in massive tables.

questions.text: Which command should be used to drop files from an internal or external stage?
questions.explanation: REMOVE is the specific DDL/DML command for purging files from staged storage.

questions.text: What metrics will the SHOW TABLES command in Snowsight provide?
questions.explanation: SHOW TABLES displays basic table metadata, including current active data usage.

questions.text: Which type of Snowflake security policy requires users to re-authenticate after a given period of inactivity?
questions.explanation: Session policies define connection lifespans and inactivity timeouts for users.

questions.text: Which statement will execute a stored procedure?
questions.explanation: Stored procedures are triggered as standalone statements using the CALL keyword.

questions.text: What are the possible values within a METADATA$ACTION column in a Snowflake stream? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Streams record row-level changes by labeling them as either an INSERT or a DELETE.
